1.label: Exam 1 (Chapters 1-6)
topics: Ch 2: Graphical Summaries (histograms, boxplots, variable types)
Ch 3: Numerical Summaries (mean, median, variance, SD, IQR, 5-number)
Ch 4: Probability (set theory, conditional prob, Bayes, independence)
Ch 5: Discrete Distributions (PMF, expected value, variance, binomial, Poisson)
Ch 6: Continuous Distributions (PDF, CDF, normal, uniform, exponential)

2.label: Exam 2 (Chapters 7-11)
topics: Ch 7: Sampling Distributions (CLT, sampling distribution of x̄)
Ch 8: Experimental Design (sampling designs, bias, randomization)
Ch 9: Confidence Intervals (CI, CB, margin of error, sample size)
Ch 10: Hypothesis Testing (Type I/II errors, power, z-test, t-test, p-value)
Ch 11: Two Sample Procedures (pooled, unpooled/Welch, paired)

final.label: Final Exam (Comprehensive, emphasis on Chapters 12-13)
topics: Ch 12: One-Way ANOVA (F-test, SSA, SSE, MSA, MSE, Tukey HSD)
Ch 13: Simple Linear Regression (correlation, least squares, diagnostics, prediction)
